Study_Notes.Details: This lesson introduces musical instruments, their names, and musical concerts. Through the story 'Mani's Drum', it explains that expensive items are not necessary to show talent, and one can achieve with available items (inclusion and individual talent). Furthermore, it fully explains the song 'Five Little Bells', English grammar applications like Pronouns (He, She, It, They) and 'There are...', Long 'i' phonics words, and the supplementary lesson 'One Bun Runs'.
Musical Instruments: Violin, Guitar, Flute, Veena, Trumpet, Shakers, Tabla, Drums, Keyboard. Stage Items: Light, Speaker, Stage, Curtain, Mike, Screen.

This is a poem about five little bells hanging in a row. The first bell said, 'Ring me slow'. The second bell said, 'Ring me fast'. The third bell said, 'Ring me soft'. The fourth bell said, 'Ring me loud'. The fifth bell said, 'Ring us together all at once'.

Mani was happy because there was a concert in class next week. The teacher asked everyone to bring the instruments they had, like Shakers and Drums. Mani really wanted to play the Drum, but his mom didn't have money to buy it. So mom gave him two sticks. In the concert, Abu played the Violin, Malar played the Keyboard, and Lisa played the Shakers. Mani played beautifully with the sticks and a few boxes he had, creating the sound 'Tada-Tada-Dum-dum'. The teacher really loved his music. Everyone clapped; the whole class danced to his music.

'He' is used to denote a male (Example: He is my brother). 'She' is used to denote a female (Example: She is my sister). 'It' is used to denote an object, animal, or bird (Example: It is my belt). 'They' is used to denote more than one person or objects (Example: They are girls / They are drums).

'There are...' is used to state the count of objects when there is more than one. Examples: There are two pencils, There are three drums, There are four boys, There are five books, There are six pens, There are ten dogs.

Letter patterns that produce the Long 'i' sound: 1. Ending in '-y': cry, fly, my, dry, fry, shy, sky, try, by. 2. Ending in '-ie': pie, tie, die, lie, mice, dried, tied, tried. 3. 'i_e' (ending in 'e' with 'i' in the middle): kite, bike, fine, mine, hide, line, rice, lime, site, dice, hike, wide, cite, file, pine. (Note: kit + e = kite, bit + e = bite, pin + e = pine, rid + e = ride, hid + e = hide, fin + e = fine).

Key words: live, last, colour, friend, eat, want, year, got, play, both. Sentence applications: 1. Crows live on trees. 2. What colour do you like? 3. I eat with my friend. 4. We want to run. 5. This year I will win. 6. I got a pen as a gift. 7. We both play with our friends.

There were many buns on a plate. One bun jumped off the plate and ran. A pig ran to catch it. A rat ran. A goat ran. A pup jumped. A frog hopped. But, a fox did not run. Seeing the fox, the bun stopped running. Immediately, the fox pounced on the bun and ate it.

1. Mani's mother gave him (sticks). 2. Abu played the (violin). 3. Mani used (boxes) as drums. 4. Class 2 was ready for the (concert). 5. (He) is Neena. 6. (It) is a box. 7. (She) has a bat. 8. (He) is Amir. 9. (It) eats an apple. 10. (She) sings a song. Story sequence: 1. We are going to play in the concert. 2. Mom, can you buy me a drum? 3. I only have the sticks. 4. The teacher loved the sound. 5. Everyone claps.

Questions.Question: Which of these is a 'Wind instrument' played by blowing air?
Questions.Explanation: A flute is an instrument played by blowing air into it.

Questions.Question: Which of these is a musical instrument played by shaking with hands?
Questions.Explanation: Shakers are instruments that produce sound when shaken in the hands.

Questions.Question: What is the meaning of the stage item 'Curtain'?
Questions.Explanation: Curtain means the screen or drape used on theatre stages.

Questions.Question: How many bells were hanging in the poem 'Five Little Bells'?
Questions.Explanation: The poem starts with 'Five little bells hanging in a row'.

Questions.Question: How did the first bell ask to be rung?
Questions.Explanation: The line in the poem is 'The first one said, 'Ring me slow''.

Questions.Question: Which bell said 'Ring me fast'?
Questions.Explanation: It is stated in the poem as 'The second one said, 'Ring me fast''.

Questions.Question: How did the fifth bell ask to be rung?
Questions.Explanation: The fifth bell asked for all the bells to be rung together at the same time.

Questions.Question: In the story 'Mani's Drum', what happy news did the teacher tell the students?
Options: A concert is going to happen
Questions.Answer: A concert is going to happen
Questions.Explanation: The teacher said, 'We are going to play in the concert next week'.

Questions.Question: What did the teacher ask the students to bring for the concert?
Options: Shakers, Drums, or whatever musical instruments they have
Questions.Answer: Shakers, Drums, or whatever musical instruments they have
Questions.Explanation: The teacher said, 'Bring your shakers and drums or whatever you have'.

Questions.Question: Which musical instrument did Mani really want to play?
Questions.Explanation: It is stated in the story, 'Mani loved to play the drums'.

Questions.Question: Why didn't Mani's mother buy him a Drum?
Options: The Drum was not in the shop
Mani didn't know how to play the Drum
Mom didn't have money to buy the Drum
The teacher said not to bring a Drum
Questions.Answer: Mom didn't have money to buy the Drum
Questions.Explanation: Mom did not have money to buy the drum.

Questions.Question: What did his mother give Mani since she couldn't buy a Drum?
Questions.Explanation: She gave Mani two nice sticks.

Questions.Question: Which musical instrument did Abu play in the class?
Questions.Explanation: Abu played the violin.

Questions.Question: Match: Which instrument did Malar play?
Questions.Explanation: Malar followed Abu with her keyboard.

Questions.Question: What did Lisa play in the concert?
Questions.Explanation: Soon, Lisa played her shakers.

Questions.Question: Since he didn't have a Drum, what items did Mani use to create music?
Questions.Explanation: It was Mani with his sticks and a few boxes.

Questions.Question: What was the sound like when Mani played on the boxes?
Questions.Explanation: When played on the boxes, the music 'Tada-Tada-Dum-dum' was produced.

Questions.Question: How did the teacher feel upon hearing Mani's playing?
Questions.Explanation: The teacher loved the sound.

Questions.Question: Which English pronoun is used to denote a boy?
Questions.Explanation: He is the word denoting a male.

Questions.Question: Which word is used to denote a girl or a woman?
Questions.Explanation: She is the word denoting a female.

Questions.Question: Which word is used to denote an animal or an object?
Questions.Explanation: It is used to denote something non-human.

Questions.Question: Which word should be used to denote more than one person or objects?
Questions.Explanation: They is a plural pronoun.

Questions.Question: Fill in the blank: That is Raju. _____ is my brother.
Questions.Explanation: Raju is male, so He is used.

Questions.Question: Fill in the blank: That is a belt. _____ is my belt.
Questions.Explanation: A belt is an inanimate object, so It is used.

Questions.Question: Complete the sentence: _____ are four boys in my bag. (Note: Textbook exercise)
Questions.Explanation: There are four boys is the correct structure.

Questions.Question: Choose the correct verb: There _____ ten dogs.
Questions.Explanation: Ten dogs is plural, so are is used.

Questions.Question: Phonics: Which of the following words ends with the letter '-y' and produces a Long 'i' sound?
Questions.Explanation: Sky, cry, fly are Long 'i' sounds ending in -y.

Questions.Question: Phonics: Which of the following words ends with the letters '-ie' and produces a Long 'i' sound?
Questions.Explanation: Tie, pie, die are Long 'i' sounds ending in -ie.

Questions.Question: Phonics: What new word is formed if the letter 'e' is added to the word 'kit'?
Questions.Explanation: According to the Magic 'e' rule, kit + e = kite (Long 'i' sound).

Questions.Question: Phonics: What new word is formed if the letter 'e' is added to the word 'pin'?
Questions.Explanation: pin + e = pine.

Questions.Question: In the story 'One Bun Runs', what jumped off the plate and ran?
Questions.Explanation: One bun jumps off the plate.

Questions.Question: What did the Pig, Rat, and Goat do to catch the bun?
Questions.Explanation: The pig runs, The rat runs, The goat runs.

Questions.Question: What did the Pup do to catch the bun?
Questions.Explanation: The pup jumps to catch it.

Questions.Question: What did the Frog do to catch the bun?
Questions.Explanation: The frog hops to catch it.

Questions.Question: Which animal did not chase the bun?
Questions.Explanation: The fox does not run.

Questions.Question: What did the bun do upon seeing the fox?
Questions.Explanation: Because the fox didn't chase it, the bun stopped quietly (The bun stops).

Questions.Question: At the end of the story, who ate the bun?
Questions.Explanation: And the fox eats the bun.

Questions.Question: Find the missing letters: d _ u _ s
Questions.Explanation: Textbook exercise: drums.

Questions.Question: Fill in the Word Wall sentence: Crows _____ on trees.
Questions.Explanation: Crows live on trees.

Questions.Question: Fill in the Word Wall sentence: What _____ do you like?
Questions.Explanation: What colour do you like?

Questions.Question: Fill in the Word Wall sentence: I ______ with my friend.
Questions.Explanation: I eat with my friend.

Questions.Question: Find the Analogy: Abu : Violin :: Lisa : _____
Questions.Explanation: Abu played the violin, Lisa played the shakers.

Questions.Question: Find the Analogy: Boy : He :: Apple : _____
Questions.Explanation: He for a male, It for an inanimate object.

Questions.Question: Find the Analogy: One girl : She :: Four boys : _____
Questions.Explanation: She/He for singular, They for plural.

Questions.Question: Find the mismatched pair (Phonics Odd one out).
Questions.Explanation: Line has a Long 'i' sound, but Boy has a different sound. The other pairs have the same sound.

Questions.Question: Life Situation Question: You really like an expensive item, but your parents don't have the money to buy it. What would you do?
Options: Like Mani did, use alternative available items and be happy
Questions.Answer: Like Mani did, use alternative available items and be happy
Questions.Explanation: We should develop the good character that expensive items are not needed for happiness and talent.

Questions.Question: Higher Order Thinking Skill (HOTS): In the story 'One Bun Runs', despite other animals chasing it, the bun wasn't caught; but how did the fox catch the bun without running?
Options: The fox acted smartly by not running, waiting quietly in one place, and catching it cunningly
Questions.Answer: The fox acted smartly by not running, waiting quietly in one place, and catching it cunningly
Questions.Explanation: The fox is a cunning animal. Knowing the bun would run if chased, it caught and ate it cunningly without chasing.

Questions.Question: Statement-Reason Question: Statement (A): Mani played excellently using the sticks and boxes he had. Reason (R): It is not necessary to have real musical instruments to showcase talent.
Options: Statement is false, Reason is true
Statement is true, Reason is false
Both Statement and Reason are true
Questions.Answer: Both Statement and Reason are true
Questions.Explanation: This is the main theme of the story. With interest, one can achieve anything.

Questions.Question: Life Situation Question: A friend in your class doesn't know how to sing well, but he knows how to draw well. How would you treat him?
Options: Tease him that he doesn't know how to sing
Understand that everyone has a unique talent and appreciate his drawing skills
Questions.Answer: Understand that everyone has a unique talent and appreciate his drawing skills
Questions.Explanation: One should understand that 'Everyone has some talent'.

Questions.Question: Higher Order Thinking Skill (HOTS): 'Everyone' (Boys and Girls) in a class danced together to a song. Which word in English grammar is used to denote 'Everyone' here?
Questions.Explanation: They is the common plural pronoun used to denote a group of more than one person, including both boys and girls.
